name: sast-configuration
description: Configure Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tools for automated vulnerability detection in application code. Use when setting up security scanning, implementing DevSecOps practices, or automating code vulnerability detection.
capabilities: Guide Semgrep configuration and custom rule creation
Explain SonarQube quality gates and security profiles
Outline CodeQL setup and query development workflows
Provide CI pipeline and pre-commit integration examples
Describe false positive tuning and performance optimization
author: wshobson
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only guidance and example commands for configuring SAST tools. All 32 static findings are false positives triggered by security-related terminology in documentation. The skill describes legitimate defensive security practices (Semgrep, SonarQube, CodeQL configuration) with no executable code, file access, network calls, or command execution. Behavior matches stated purpose of providing SAST configuration guidance.
valueStatement: You need consistent SAST tool setup across your projects. This skill provides guidance for configuring Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL with custom rules and CI integration.
userTitle: Configure SAST tools for secure code scanning
limitations: Does not run scans or install tools automatically
Does not access your repositories or modify CI settings
Does not guarantee compliance certification outcomes
Requires you to configure tool-specific credentials

useCases.title: SAST baseline rollout
useCases.description: Plan and configure Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL for an initial organization baseline scan.

useCases.title: CI pipeline integration
useCases.description: Add SAST checks to GitHub Actions or GitLab CI with clear failure gates.

useCases.title: Custom rule authoring
useCases.description: Create targeted rules to detect risky patterns in a specific codebase.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner setup
promptTemplates.scenario: New SAST setup for a small repo
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a basic Semgrep scan for a Python project and suggest a minimal CI step.

promptTemplates.title: Pipeline hardening
promptTemplates.prompt: Define a CI workflow that blocks merges on critical SAST findings and keeps noise low.

promptTemplates.title: Rule tuning
promptTemplates.prompt: Suggest rule tuning and path exclusions to reduce false positives in a large monorepo.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced multi-tool
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a plan to combine Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL with clear ownership and reporting.

bestPractices: Start with a baseline scan and prioritize critical issues first
Document suppressions and review them regularly
Cache dependencies and use incremental scans for speed
antiPatterns: Blocking merges on low severity findings
Scanning generated or vendor code by default

faq.question: Which platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: It provides guidance for Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL across common CI platforms.

faq.question: What are the limits of this skill?
faq.answer: It does not execute scans or modify your CI configuration directly.

faq.question: Can it integrate with my existing CI pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes, it offers examples for GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and pre-commit hooks.

faq.question: Is my data accessed or stored?
faq.answer: No, the skill content is static documentation and does not access files or transmit data.

faq.question: How do I troubleshoot noisy results?
faq.answer: Use rule tuning, path exclusions, and documented suppressions to reduce noise.

faq.question: How does it compare to using one tool only?
faq.answer: It helps plan a multi-tool approach for stronger coverage and defense in depth.
